name: My ears are ringing after years at the factory, what do I do first for a workers comp claim?

mainEntity.name: My ears are ringing after years at the factory, what do I do first for a workers comp claim?

acceptedAnswer.text: Report to your employer now, get an audiogram, and document exposure—delays hurt claims.[1][2]
acceptedAnswer.description:
**Report the hearing loss to your employer immediately and get a medical evaluation from an audiologist.**

Workers' compensation covers occupational hearing loss from prolonged noise exposure, but prompt reporting is crucial to avoid claim denials.[1][2] Delays can weaken your case due to challenges proving work-related causation.

**Key factors:**
- Document noise levels and exposure duration via job records or coworker statements.
- Obtain an audiogram showing Standard Threshold Shift (STS) of 25 dB or more.[3][6]
- Note if employer provided inadequate protection.
- Pre-existing conditions may complicate proof.

See a workers' comp attorney to file properly and gather evidence like OSHA logs.[2] Act within your state's statute of limitations, often 1-2 years from diagnosis.[1]

Consulting a specialist early strengthens your position for benefits.

description: Report to your employer now, get an audiogram, and document exposure—delays hurt claims.[1][2]
